title: Cisco Nexus NX-API REST Interface Schema
description: JSON Schema for Cisco Nexus switch interface managed objects as exposed through the NX-API REST DME model. Covers physical interfaces (l1PhysIf), SVI interfaces (sviIf), VLAN bridge domains (l2BD), and interface statistics (rmonEtherStats). Based on the Cisco Nexus 3000 and 9000 Series NX-API REST SDK documentation.

distinguishedName.description: Globally unique identifier for a managed object in the NX-OS Management Information Tree (MIT). Composed of a series of relative names from the root to the object.

interfaceId.description: Physical Ethernet interface identifier matching the output of show interface brief on the Nexus switch.

vlanEncap.description: VLAN fabric encapsulation identifier used by the NX-OS DME model to reference VLAN bridge domains.

sviId.description: Switch Virtual Interface identifier combining the string vlan with a numeric VLAN ID.

adminState.description: Administrative state indicating whether the interface or feature is operationally enabled or disabled by the administrator.

operState.description: Operational state reflecting the actual runtime status of the interface as detected by the system.

layerMode.description: Operating layer of a physical interface. Layer2 enables switching functions. Layer3 enables routing functions.

switchportMode.description: Switchport mode determining how the interface handles VLAN tagged and untagged traffic.

interfaceSpeed.description: Configured line speed of the physical interface. Use auto for speed auto-negotiation.

duplexMode.description: Duplex mode of the physical interface. Use auto for duplex auto-negotiation.

portMedium.description: Port medium type controlling how the SVI interface handles broadcast traffic.

l1PhysIf.title: Layer 1 Physical Interface (l1PhysIf)
l1PhysIf.description: Represents a physical Ethernet interface on a Cisco Nexus switch. This managed object maps to the l1PhysIf class in the NX-OS DME model. The distinguished name format is sys/intf/phys-[{id}]. Configuration is applied via POST to /api/mo/sys/intf.json and state is retrieved via GET to /api/mo/sys/intf/phys-[{id}].json.

dn.description: Distinguished name of the physical interface (e.g., sys/intf/phys-[eth1/1])

id.description: Interface identifier matching the show interface brief output

adminSt.description: Administrative state (up enables the interface, down shuts it down)

operSt.description: Current operational state of the interface (read-only)

operStQual.description: Qualification reason when the operational state is down (read-only)

layer.description: Layer mode (Layer2 for switching, Layer3 for routing)

mode.description: Switchport mode when operating in Layer2

accessVlan.description: Access VLAN assignment for access mode ports

trunkVlans.description: Trunk allowed VLANs specified as a range. Supports comma-separated values, ranges (1-100), and incremental add (+50) or remove (-50) operations.

nativeVlan.description: Native VLAN for untagged frames on trunk ports

speed.description: Configured line speed of the physical interface

duplex.description: Duplex mode of the physical interface

mtu.description: Maximum transmission unit in bytes. Valid range is 576 to 9216.

descr.description: Human-readable description of the interface purpose and connectivity.

snmpTrapSt.description: Whether SNMP trap notifications are sent for link-state changes on this interface.

bw.description: Administrative bandwidth in kilobits per second used by routing protocols for metric calculation.

delay.description: Administrative delay in tens of microseconds used by routing protocols for metric calculation.

modTs.description: Timestamp of the last configuration modification (read-only)

children.description: Child managed objects including port channel membership, statistics, and hardware details

sviIf.title: Switch Virtual Interface (sviIf)
sviIf.description: Represents a Switch Virtual Interface (SVI) providing Layer 3 routing for a VLAN. This managed object maps to the sviIf class in the NX-OS DME model. The distinguished name format is sys/intf/svi-[{id}]. Requires the interface-vlan feature to be enabled (fmInterfaceVlan adminSt=enabled). Configuration is applied via POST to /api/mo/sys/intf/svi-[{id}].json.

dn.description: Distinguished name of the SVI (e.g., sys/intf/svi-[vlan100])

id.description: SVI identifier in the format vlan{id}

bw.description: Administrative bandwidth in kilobits per second (1-400000000).

mac.description: MAC address override for the SVI in MM:MM:MM:SS:SS:SS or M.M.M format.

medium.description: Port medium type (broadcast for multi-access, p2p for point-to-point)

autostate.description: When true, the SVI tracks the operational state of its member ports. When false, the SVI stays up regardless of port state.

carDel.description: Carrier delay in seconds before reporting a link-down event.

loadIntvl1.description: Load interval in seconds for computing interface utilization statistics.

inbMgmt.description: Whether the SVI is used for inband management.

snmpTrap.description: Whether SNMP trap notifications are sent for link-state changes.

modTs.description: Timestamp of the last configuration modification (read-only)

children.description: Child managed objects including IPv4/IPv6 address configuration

l2BD.title: Layer 2 Bridge Domain (l2BD)
l2BD.description: Represents a VLAN bridge domain on a Cisco Nexus switch. This managed object maps to the l2BD class in the NX-OS DME model. The distinguished name format is sys/bd/bd-[{fabEncap}]. Configuration is applied via POST to /api/mo/sys/bd.json and state is retrieved via GET to /api/mo/sys/bd/bd-[{fabEncap}].json.

dn.description: Distinguished name of the bridge domain (e.g., sys/bd/bd-[vlan-100])

pcTag.description: Policy control tag / default classId for unknown unicast traffic.

adminSt.description: Administrative state of the VLAN bridge domain.

modTs.description: Timestamp of the last configuration modification (read-only)

children.description: Child managed objects including VLAN member ports and statistics

rmonEtherStats.title: RMON Ethernet Statistics (rmonEtherStats)
rmonEtherStats.description: Ethernet RMON statistics counters for a physical interface. These are read-only child objects of l1PhysIf that refresh every 30-40 seconds. Retrieved via GET to /api/mo/sys/intf/phys-[{id}].json?query-target=subtree&target-subtree-class=rmonEtherStats.

dropEvents.description: Total packet drop events due to resource limitations

rmonIfHCIn.title: RMON High-Capacity Inbound Counters (rmonIfHCIn)
rmonIfHCIn.description: High-capacity 64-bit inbound traffic counters for a physical interface. Read-only child of l1PhysIf.

rmonIfHCOut.title: RMON High-Capacity Outbound Counters (rmonIfHCOut)
rmonIfHCOut.description: High-capacity 64-bit outbound traffic counters for a physical interface. Read-only child of l1PhysIf.
